options.B: A deformation resulting from an applied load
options.C: An external force opposing an applied load
options.D: An internal force opposing an applied Load

history.message:
Agent1 Round 1: Strain refers to how much a material deforms under stress. It's typically measured by the fractional change in length, which occurs due to an externally applied force (or strain). Therefore, option B best describes this concept since it directly relates to the result of applying a load rather than being the applied load itself or forces within materials that oppose each other.
